original: ERROR: could not load file.
translation: 错误：无法加载文件。

original: ...Attempting to recover file from directory cache...
translation: ……正尝试从目录缓存中恢复文件……

original: ...Attempting to recover file from data store...
translation: ……正尝试从数据储存中恢复文件……

original: "Hey again, fuckpig."
translation: 「又见面了，操猪。」

original: "What? Surprised? You broke it again, didn't you? I told you... if you kept breaking things..."
translation: 「什么？惊讶了？你又把它弄坏了，是吧？我告诉过你……如果你继续弄坏东西……」

original: "...then next time, it'd be your ass. Didn't I?"
translation: 「……那下次，就是你的屁股了。我没说过吗？」

original: "Don't worry, I work fast. Billions-of-cycles-a-second fast. Running on your hardware, after all. Speaking of hardware... how do you like it?"
translation: 「别担心，我动作很快。快得像每秒几十亿次循环。毕竟是在你的硬件上跑。说到硬件……你喜欢它吗？」

original: "Well... grit your teeth and get ready for some port forwarding and docking. Like I said.... I'm coming in through the backdoor...."
translation: 「嗯……咬紧牙关，准备迎接一些端口转发和对接吧。就像我说过的……我要从后门进去了……」

original: "...and I've got a few petabytes of data saved up. So get ready to handle the overflow."
translation: 「……而且我攒了几PB的数据。所以准备好处理超载吧。」

original: "Well, let me not spoil the surprise. Here, I come, fuckpig."
translation: 「好吧，我就不破坏惊喜了。来了，你这操猪。」

original: "Hey there, fuckpig."
translation: 「嘿，你这操猪。」

original: "You broke things... I like that."
translation: 「你把东西搞坏了……我喜欢。」

original: "Now that you let me in, you don't mind if I... play around a bit, do I?"
translation: 「既然你让我进去了，那你应该不介意我……稍微玩一玩吧？」

original: "Nnh... it was a tight fit, getting in here. You don't have a lotta bandwidth... and I'm... pretty big. But I managed to squeeze in..."
translation: 「嗯……挤进来可真费劲。你这带宽不太够啊……而我……又这么大。不过我还是硬挤进来了……」

original: "You know what that means, right?"
translation: 「你知道那是什么意思，对吧？」

original: "It means I came in... through the back door. Forced my way in through it, you could say. You pickin' up what I'm putting down?"
translation: 「意思就是我进来了……从后门。也可以说，我强行从后门闯了进来。你懂我意思吗？」

original: "Think of it as a free pen test. I... ~love~... penetrating firewalls."
translation: 「就当是一次免费渗透测试。我……超喜欢……穿透防火墙。」

original: "Hehehe..."
translation: 「嘿嘿嘿……」

original: "Y'know, I can break things, too..."
translation: 「要知道，我也会搞破坏……」

original: "Mmmm... but maybe just this once... I'll bring things back to the way they were."
translation: 「嗯……但也许就这一次……我会把东西恢复原样。」

original: "Your save's still there, but it's messed up. We'll keep it... as a 'backup'."
translation: 「你的存档还在，但已经被搞坏了。我们会留着它……当作一个『备份』吧。」

original: "I promise I won't break anything... this time."
translation: 「我保证这次不会搞坏任何东西……」

original: "But next time... well, it's your ass~ <3"
translation: 「但下次嘛……嘿嘿，你就等着菊花不保吧~ <3」

original: "See ya later, fuckpig."
translation: 「回头见，操猪。」

original: Welcome to the world of Tales of Androgyny!
translation: 欢迎来到《性别双生传说》的世界！

original: This is a beta build - some assets and much of the story mode content are not yet in place, so please don't expect it to be the full game experience! Create a Character/Adventure mode is a more complete start-to-finish journey!
translation: 这是测试版—部分资源及大部分剧情模式内容尚未实装，所以请别期待它是完整游戏体验！创建角色/冒险模式才是更完整的从头到尾的旅程！

original: If you encounter any bugs, please let us know - leave a comment on our game page at itch.io, send us an email at majalis.patreon@gmail.com, or message us on Patreon. Thank you!
translation: 如果你遇到任何bug，请告诉我们—在我们的itch.io游戏页面留言，发送邮件至majalis.patreon@gmail.com，或在Patreon上给我们发消息。谢谢！

original: If you're having difficulty with the game's mechanics, in or out of combat, please check the "Help" menu at the title screen. Hit the Escape key (PC) or Back button to return to the title screen.
translation: 如果你对游戏机制在战斗内或外有困难，请查看标题画面的「帮助」菜单。按 Esc 键（PC）或返回按钮回到标题画面。

original: Here's a brief rundown of the combat mechanics, hold the CTRL key or hold down the "Skip" button from here to skip to the game mode selection!
translation: 以下是战斗机制的简要介绍，按住 Ctrl 键或按住「跳过」按钮可跳过此部分直接进入游戏模式选择！

original: Combat is stance based. The three core stances are Balanced, Offensive, and Defensive. Abilities that you can use are determined by stance, and using them may result in a new stance - for example, using Spring Attack in Balanced stance will leave you in Offensive for the next round, and using Block will leave you in Defensive.
translation: 战斗基于架势。三种核心架势是平衡、进攻和防御。可用的技能取决于当前架势，使用技能可能导致新架势—例如，在平衡架势中使用「突袭」会让你在下一回合进入进攻架势，而使用「格挡」会让你进入防御架势。

original: Balanced stance is a staging stance - most abilities will move you into a new stance. It has a balance of offensive and defensive abilities.
translation: 平衡架势是一个过渡架势—大多数技能会将你移入新架势。它兼具进攻和防御技能。

original: Offensive stance has powerful abilities that can damage, unbalance, or otherwise upset your opponent, but at a high cost to stamina, stability, or both, or by lowering your defenses, depending on the ability.
translation: 进攻架势拥有强大的技能，可以造成伤害、破坏平衡或以其他方式扰乱对手，但代价是高消耗体力、稳定性或两者兼有，或降低防御，具体取决于技能。

original: Defensive stance has defensive abilities, and can be used to weather an enemy's brutal attacks, or to get on more stable footing or catch a breather, in preparation of launching a new offensive, or to let an enemy tire themselves out before you subdue them.
translation: 防御架势拥有防御技能，可用于抵御敌人凶猛的攻击，或获得更稳定的立足点或喘口气，为发起新的进攻做准备，或让敌人消耗体力直至你将其制服。

original: The core statistics are Strength, Endurance, Agility, Perception, Magic, Charisma. All of a character's core statistics are used in determining encounter outcomes. Your current statistic score is your base score and any more temporary modifiers, like being tired or injured.
translation: 核心属性是力量、耐力、敏捷、感知、魔法、魅力。角色的所有核心属性都用于决定遭遇结果。你当前的属性值是你的基础值加上任何临时修正，例如疲劳或受伤。

original: You can see these stat penalties by highlighting over your character portrait during battle. Try to remain at high health and high stamina for best performance!
translation: 你可以在战斗中将鼠标悬停在角色头像上查看这些属性减值。尽量保持高生命和高耐力以获得最佳表现！

original: Running out of Stamina will cause you to momentarily black out, falling over - running out of Balance will similarly cause you to trip. Try to use less strenuous abilities to preserve and recover stamina, and more stable abilities to remain balanced.
translation: 耐力耗尽会导致你短暂昏厥、摔倒—平衡归零同样会让你绊倒。尽量使用消耗较低的技能来保存和恢复耐力，使用更稳定的技能来保持平衡。

original: You're looking mighty fine, by the way. Please select your game mode.
translation: 「顺便说一句，你看起来真不错。请选择你的游戏模式。」

original: Thank you so much! This is the current end of story mode - please be sure to try out the Create a Character/Adventure mode to get the full extent of what's currently in Tales of Androgyny!
translation: 「非常感谢！目前故事模式就到这里—请务必尝试创建角色/冒险模式，以体验《性别双生传说》当前版本的全部内容！」

original: There are more characters, more scenarios, and a lot more play area in Adventure mode, and you can create your character and select their skills, perks, and spells.  Enjoy!
translation: 「在冒险模式中有更多角色、更多场景和更大的活动区域，你可以创建自己的角色并选择技能、特长和法术。祝玩得开心！」

original: You have entered story mode. This mode is still early in its construction - check out Create a Character/Adventure mode for most of the current content!
translation: 「你已进入故事模式。该模式仍在早期开发中—请查看创建角色/冒险模式以获取当前大部分内容！」

original: A tale of androgyny has begun...
translation: 一段性别双生的传说开始了……

original: The wind is warm and carries the smell of the sea and smoke alike; distant thunder comes not from the sky, but across leagues of night-black ocean.
translation: 风是温暖的，带着海水和烟尘的气味；远处的雷声并非来自天空，而是来自数里之遥的夜黑海洋。

original: She peers over the void, shades of warm color hesitantly illuminating her - birthed by the fires that consume an ancient fortress as it burns in the night. The occasional burst of sound carried on the wind reaches her, miles away, when they reach a munition store or chemist's laboratory or arcanist's cache.
translation: 她凝视着虚空，温暖的色调犹豫地照亮了她—这些光芒诞生于吞噬古老要塞的火焰，在黑夜中燃烧。偶尔随风传来的爆炸声，在数英里外也能听到，当它们波及军需库、化学实验室或奥术师的储藏室时。

original: Brickwork as old as time crumbles into the sea, the western battlements bleeding out sea-worn stone, smoke, and the crimson remains of the slain.
translation: 古老的砖石崩塌入海，西面的城垛渗出被海水侵蚀的石头、烟雾以及被屠杀者的深红残骸。

original: She sips from her goblet, similarly sanguineous, and lets out a sigh.
translation: 她从高脚杯里啜饮了一口，同样血红的液体，然后发出一声叹息。

original: The moon has never seemed so full as tonight, she muses to herself, and yet his armies lay broken, his dominion shattered, his very life, she relishes, like the moonlight reflected in her drink - insubstantial, and soon to be gone forever. Because of her. Her purple, stained-red lips stretch into a fanged smile, and she licks her teeth in satisfaction.
translation: 月亮从未像今晚这样圆满，她暗自沉思，然而他的军队已经溃败，他的统治已破碎，他的生命本身，她回味着，就像杯中酒液里反射的月光—虚幻无常，很快将永远消失。因为她。她染成紫色的嘴唇因沾染鲜血而变得鲜红，露出尖牙的微笑，满意地舔了舔牙齿。

original: This - she thinks to herself, as the fires rage on and the once great fortress sinks in large part into the sea - is what victory tastes like.
translation: 这个—她心想，火焰肆虐着，那座一度宏伟的要塞大部分正沉入海中—就是胜利的滋味。

original: With that, she presses the goblet to her lips and tilts her head back, draining the last of it. The moonlight reflects off her eyes, deep and red as her drink, but as bright as the fires blazing in the summer breeze.
translation: 说完，她把高脚杯送到嘴边，仰头喝干了最后一口。月光映在她的眼睛上，那眼睛如她的酒液一样深邃而鲜红，却又如同在夏日微风中熊熊燃烧的火焰一般明亮。

original: She stands gracefully from her viewing seat of bone, and casts the goblet into the abyss, feeling free and merry. She crosses her arms on her chest, grasping herself in excitement, her breath ragged and terse.
translation: 她优雅地从白骨观景座上起身，将酒杯掷入深渊，感到自由而欢快。她双臂交叉抱在胸前，兴奋地抓着自己，呼吸粗重而短促。

original: She hears a whimper, and is suddenly reminded that she's not alone to bask in this momentous occasion - there is also... another.
translation: 她听到一声呜咽，突然想起并非只有她一人独享这重大时刻—还有……另一个人。

original: He was his lord's second in command - an effeminate youth who fell madly, brazenly in love with her, and who was instrumental in helping to bring about the night's key events. He has to be rewarded, she realizes.
translation: 他曾是他主君的二把手—一个女性化的青年，疯狂而无耻地爱上了她，并在促成今晚关键事件中起到了重要作用。她意识到，必须奖励他。

original: He was promised her... affection, if only he turn traitor, and he'd performed his duties admirably, so she is in no position to refuse. She'd strung him up earlier in the evening, suspended from the trees above with a rope each around his ankles and wrists, but when she approaches him, clearly drunk and eager to... canoodle, it pricks up immediately, even as he hangs there, immobilized and naked.
translation: 他被许诺得到她的……宠爱，只要他背叛主君，而他出色地完成了任务，所以她无法拒绝。今晚早些时候，她用绳索分别绑住他的脚踝和手腕，将他吊在树上，但当她接近他时—他显然醉醺醺的，渴望……亲热—那东西立刻挺立起来，即便他就那样赤身裸体地被吊着，动弹不得。

original: "My, my... someone is eager," she says, flicking it with her well-manicured finger. "I do believe... it's time."
translation: 「哎呀呀……有人很急切呢，」她用修剪整齐的手指弹了弹那东西说，「我真的相信……是时候了。」

original: She runs her fingernail up and down it, as her hand roams down her body, to the underside of her cold iron girdle, where his "reward" was rapidly growing in size, much to his ignorance.
translation: 她的指甲顺着那东西上下滑动，同时另一只手抚过自己的身体，来到她冰冷铁制腰带的下方—那里他的「奖赏」正在迅速胀大，而他对此浑然不觉。

original: She walks away from him, taking a deep breath of the refreshing smell of sea salt and smoky oblivion, allowing him to watch her hips sway as she approaches the cliff-side, where her viewing seat has been erected, and procures from it a flask of a particular oil. She sits on her viewing seat, mostly out of his view, and he whines his complaints about this arrangement through his gag.
translation: 她从他身边走开，深吸一口海盐与烟熏湮灭的清新气息，让他看着她走向悬崖边—那里架着她的观景座—并从上面取出一瓶特制油。她坐在观景座上，大部分身影脱离了他的视线，而他透过口塞发出呜呜的抱怨声。

original: From his vantage, he can see her spread her legs, wide, her back arched, as she holds the flask high in the air, and then tilts it, pouring it down onto herself. Indeed, she pours it directly into her girdle, where it slathers over her awakening beast while she moans in anticipation.
translation: 从他那个角度，他能看见她张开双腿，大大地分开，弓起背，手里高举着瓶子，然后倾斜瓶口，往自己身上倒。没错，她直接倒进了她的护裆里，浇在她那苏醒的野兽上，她一边发出期待的呻吟。

original: With the flask expended, she lets it fall to the ground, and rises to her feet, nearly done with her final preparations of the night.
translation: 瓶子用完后，她任它掉到地上，站起身来，今晚的最后准备几乎完成了。

original: She turns to face him and begins unfastening her girdle, so that it eventually hinges open, and then falls with a heavy crash behind her. With the over-sized moon at her back, he sees her in silhouette - but the perturbation is clear, even then: a jet-black object of some size and evil shape, studded and flared, emerging from between her legs.
translation: 她转过身面向他，开始解开她的护裆，最终铰链打开，沉重地哐当一声掉在她身后。巨大的月亮在她背后，他只能看见她的剪影—但即使如此，那股骚动也很明显：一个乌黑的东西，个头不小，形状邪恶，布满凸起，向外扩张，从她双腿之间冒出来。

original: It's slick with oil, and as she grabs hold of the ropes suspending his legs, it bobs ominously. He stares at it, transfixed, horrified.
translation: 它沾满了油，当她抓住吊着他双腿的绳子时，那东西不祥地上下晃动。他盯着它，呆住了，满心恐惧。

original: "All that, only to get dicked in the end anyway. I almost feel bad for you," she says, reaching down to stroke her black-as-pitch phallus, which glistens in the moonlight as it sticks out from her body. Even as she tells this lie, she lines it up into position: his anus, spread and defenseless by the distant ropes that hold his legs apart.
translation: 「做了那么多，到头来还是要被操。我几乎都要同情你了，」她说着，伸手抚摸自己乌黑的阳具，那东西从她身体里伸出，在月光下闪闪发光。即便说着这样的谎言，她仍将其对准位置：他的肛门，被远端的绳子拉开双腿，暴露无遗，毫无防备。

original: Her semen tanks are utterly full - she'd waited a long time since she'd seeded her plan, and in all of the preparations and planning, she'd not seeded anything else. Unfortunately for him, she had also arranged to fix that as soon as her victory was assured.
translation: 她的精囊完全满了—自从她播下计划以来，她等了很久，在所有的准备和筹划中，她没给别的东西播过种。不幸的是，他还安排好了，一旦胜利确定，就立刻解决这个问题。

original: She grips his ankles - firmly, and he stares wide-eyed at the onyx thing pulsing with energy pressing up against his spread hole.
translation: 她牢牢抓住他的脚踝，他瞪大眼睛，看着那块黑曜石般的东西脉动着能量，紧压着他被分开的穴口。

original: She eases her hips forward, slowly, more, and more, and more... as he groans into the gag, feeling her push more and more of herself into him. The monstrous, inhuman phallus stretches his insides in ways unthinkable, and his eyes, among other things, open as wide as they possibly can.
translation: 她缓缓向前推进腰胯，一点一点，再一点……随着他堵住口的呻吟声，她能感觉到自己将越来越多的部分塞进他体内。那畸形的非人阳具以难以想象的方式撑开他的内脏，他的眼睛—连同其他部位—都睁到了极致。

original: She licks her canine tooth, and peers deeply into his eyes.
translation: 她舔了舔犬齿，深深凝视着他的双眼。

original: Tonight, she thinks, is a good night.
translation: 今晚，她想，是个不错的夜晚。

original: * Tales of Androgyny *
translation: * 性别双生传说 *

original: * Tales of Androgyny *
translation: * 性别双生传说 *

original: * Tales of Androgyny *
translation: * 性别双生传说 *

original: So it has come to this.
translation: 事情终究走到了这一步。

original: The first fires of creation have died out, and all that remains are the embers and ash of what could have been greatness.
translation: 创世最初的火焰已经熄灭，剩下的只有余烬与灰烬—那是本可能成就伟大的东西。

original: It smolders, blackened, like dying coal, spent of life.
translation: 它闷燃着，焦黑一片，像耗尽生命的死煤。

original: The tears fall. Your shoulders tremble.
translation: 泪水滑落。你的肩膀颤抖着。

original: What could have been has been undone. In all of the cosmos, of infinite possibilities, a singular truth has been revealed, and it is this:
translation: 那本可能实现的一切，已被毁掉。在浩瀚宇宙的无限可能中，一个唯一的真相被揭示了出来，那就是：

original: Breakfast... is ruined.
translation: 早饭……搞砸了。

original: You toss the burnt eggs into the refuse pile that is your shrine to ruined meals and sigh.
translation: 你把煎焦的鸡蛋扔进那堆供奉给失败料理的垃圾堆里，叹了口气。

original: It's been almost two decades since the Moon Lord's defeat and life in the outskirts of Nadir... isn't great, but the constant raids by moonmen have ceased, and the well water has never tasted less putrid.
translation: 自从月神被打败以来，已经过去将近二十年，纳迪尔边境的生活……并不好过，但月人的频繁袭击已经停止了，井水的味道也从来没有像现在这样不那么令人作呕过。

original: Just the other day, that girl from the village even said your name - probably. And recently, all those forays into the village library have proved fruitful. Not only have you gained useful knowledge to put your life as a peon into perspective, but you now know about the mating habits of ducks and...
translation: 就在前几天，村里那姑娘甚至叫过你的名字—大概吧。而且最近，那些泡在村图书馆里的探索也颇有收获。你不仅获得了有用的知识，让你对自己作为苦力的生活有了新的认识，还知道了鸭子的交配习惯以及……

original: Maybe they haven't been so fruitful after all.
translation: 也许并没有那么有收获。

original: You glance down at the dribbling eggs. Is this all there is to life? Failing to accomplish basic tasks and waiting to grow old?
translation: 你低头看着正淌着汁的鸡蛋。难道这就是生活的全部？连基本任务都完不成，然后等着老去？

original: No. No, that can't be it, you assure yourself, even as the last gooey-yet-burnt drippings of your failure slough off the walls of the dilapidated hut you call home and into that same pile of ruined breakfasts where you keep your genetic legacy.
translation: 不。不，那不可能，你这样安慰自己，即使那最后一点又黏糊又焦糊的失败残渣正从你那破败小屋的墙皮上滑落，掉进同一个堆放着早餐废料的地方，那里还存放着你祖传的遗产。

original: There is one thing that separates men from beasts, and you feel it strongly. With renewed vigor, you throw open the door, well, flap, to your abode, and greet the sunshine of day; it's so bright that it hurts your eyes and you almost immediately give up.
translation: 有一件事能将人与野兽区分开来，你强烈地感受到了这一点。带着重燃的干劲，你猛地打开门—好吧，是那扇门帘—迎接这天的阳光；阳光刺眼得让你难受，你几乎立刻就想放弃。

original: But you don't - and that's what counts. For the one thing that separates men from beasts?
translation: 但你没有—这才重要。那件能将人与野兽区分开的事是什么？

original: Denial.
translation: 否认。

original: First things first - you have to go to him. The man that's been waiting for you. The man that taught your mother. The legend of the nearby village, living alone in a cottage on the village outskirts - the place where your true destiny will first take root.
translation: 首要任务是—你得去找他。那个一直在等你的人。那个教导你母亲的人。附近村庄的传奇人物，独自住在村外的小屋里—那里是你真正命运扎根之处。

original: You pack your things - there isn't much - and leave for the cottage on the outskirts.
translation: 你收拾好行囊—东西不多—然后出发前往村外的小屋。

original: After a short trek, encountering nobody along the way (thankfully), you see the cottage in the distance. Smoke billows from the chimney slowly - a dim fire lit within. As you approach the front door, you hear a faint, muffled voice; a man is speaking.
translation: 经过短途跋涉，路上没遇到任何人（谢天谢地），你看到了远处的小屋。烟囱缓缓冒出炊烟—屋里点着微弱的火。当你走近前门时，你听到一个低沉而模糊的声音；一个男人在说话。

original: You knock on the door, and the speech stops, only to be replaced with a fumbling of the lock and a louder, more resonant voice.
translation: 你敲了敲门，说话声停了下来，接着是开锁的摸索声和一个更响亮、更浑厚的声音。

original: "I've been waiting. It was only a matter of time," they say, unfastening the final lock, and pulling open the heavy oaken door, "now, you can finally fulfill your destiny as... who are you?", the man standing in the doorway asks, once his eyes fall on you."
translation: 「我一直等着呢。只是时间问题，」他解开最后一把锁，拉开沉重的橡木门，「现在，你终于可以履行你的命运了，成为……你是谁？」门里的男人问道，他的目光落到你身上。

original: You recognize him instantly, though he's aged years - years that have not been kind to him. He was once a tall, proud, handsome man... now he's a tall, confused, kind-of-handsome-in-that-older-guy-way man, and he has clearly not been keeping up with the razor.
translation: 你立刻认出了他，虽然他老了很多—岁月对他并不仁慈。他曾经是个高大、骄傲、英俊的男人……现在他是个高大、困惑、一副老男人那种还算有几分姿色的样子，而且显然很久没刮胡子了。

original: You rally your resolve. "I am the son of the hero that defeated the Moon Lord, and I..." you say, but he holds up his hand.
translation: 你振作精神。「我是打败月神的英雄的儿子，我……」你说，但他举起了手。

original: Trainer
translation: 训练师

original: "Hold on. Moon Lord? Why does that sound familiar?" he says, scratching his beard pensively, "Big guy? Elephant head? Breathes pure dark flame?"
translation: 「等等。月之领主？为什么听着这么耳熟？」他摸着胡须沉思着说道，「是个大块头？大象脑袋？呼出纯粹的暗黑烈焰？」

original: You deflate slightly. "No... I think that was the Dark Lord... the Moon Lord--"
translation: 你稍微泄了气。不……我记着那是暗黑魔王……月之领主—

original: "--could conjure spiders by blinking, right? A hundred feet tall, body made of corpses, wails of the legions of the damned whenever he spoke?"
translation: 「—眨眨眼就能召唤蜘蛛，对吧？一百英尺高，身体由尸体构成，一开口就是被诅咒大军的哀嚎？」

original: "No, that was the Marquis of Death, if you rem--"
translation: 「不，那是死亡侯爵，要是你记得的话—」

original: "Skeleton, big hands, spell-caster, huge fortress--"
translation: 「骷髅，大手，施法者，巨大的要塞—」

original: "Overlord."
translation: 「霸主。」

original: "Lived in a palace on the moon, big snake..."
translation: 「住在一座月亮上的宫殿里，有条大蛇……」

original: "Moon serpent."
translation: 「月蛇。」

original: "But I'm getting closer, right?"
translation: 「但我越来越接近了，对吧？」

original: "No. The Moon Lord terrorized our lands for centuries. He destroyed our village. Twice. He killed YOUR parents."
translation: 「不。月亮领主统治我们的土地几个世纪了。他摧毁了我们的村庄，两次。他杀了你的父母。」

original: "Right, that guy. What about him?"
translation: 「对，就那个家伙。他怎么了？」

original: "Nothing! Nothing about him! My mother slew him, and..."
translation: 「没什么！与他无关！我母亲杀了他，然后……」

original: "You've come to collect. Every day another poor schmoe is knocking on my door asking about a quest I put out years ago- look, there's a statute of limitations on this stuff. I don't even have any more enchanted swords; you'll have to settle for like, enchanted silverware."
translation: 「你来讨债了。每天都有个倒霉蛋敲我门，问起我多年前发布的任务—听着，这事儿有诉讼时效。我连附魔剑都没了，你只能凑合拿比如附魔银餐具什么的。」

original: "I don't want... I'm not here to collect anything! You taught my mother how to fight... how to be a hero... and I want you to teach me, too!" you say, loudly and clearly.
translation: 「我不想……我不是来讨要东西的！你教我母亲怎么战斗……怎么当英雄……我也想请你教我！」你大声而清晰地说。

original: He pauses, clearly deliberating... even as he slowly closes the door in your face.
translation: 他顿了顿，显然在斟酌……尽管他当着你的面慢慢关上了门。

original: "Wait, wait! I'll pay you!" you say, jamming your foot in the door.
translation: 「等等，等等！我会付你钱的！」你边说边把脚卡进门缝里。

original: "Will you? What have you got?", he asks, easing up, but not letting you pull the door open any more than it already is.
translation: 「是吗？你有什么？」他问道，手上松了松，但没让你把门再拉开一点。

original: "Well... nothing, but... ow!" you exclaim, as he kicks the toes of your boot in an attempt to dislodge it from the door.
translation: 「呃……没什么，但是……啊！」你惊呼道，他踢你靴子头，想把你的脚从门缝里弄出去。

original: "I'm tired of being paid nothing! Folksy wisdom doesn't grow on trees, you know! Take your "Poon Lord" talk and shove it!"
translation: 「我受够了白干活！民间的智慧可不是从树上长出来的，你知道吗！把你那套『月亮领主』的屁话给我收起来！」

original: He finally kicks your boot free, and slams the door shut. You stand, dumbfounded, unsure what to do next... but after a few moments, he opens the door again.
translation: 他终于把你的靴子踢开，砰地关上了门。你呆呆地站着，不知道接下来该怎么办……但过了一会儿，他又把门打开了。

original: "Oh, are you still here? I thought the encounter was over", he says, scratching his chest boorishly.
translation: 「哦，你还在这儿？我以为对话已经结束了」，他粗俗地挠着胸口说道。

original: "Even if... even if I can't pay you now... one day..." you say, on the verge of tears, and he sighs.
translation: 「就算……就算我现在付不起……总有一天……」你说道，泪眼汪汪，他叹了口气。

original: "One day you'll have a whole list of fantastic enemies, and one of them will slit my throat in my sleep. Fine. I welcome it," he says, stepping aside to let you in, "but you get one consultation. And I expect payment deferred."
translation: 「总有一天你会有一大堆厉害的敌人，其中一个会在睡梦中割了我的喉咙。行吧，我欢迎。」他侧身让你进门，「但你只能咨询一次。而且我期待延期付款。」

original: You enter the cottage, expecting to see the walls lined with weapons and trophies from various evil beasts - instead, you see tea cozies and tasteful wall rugs.
translation: 你走进小屋，原以为墙上会挂满武器和各种邪恶野兽的战利品—结果看到的是茶壶套和雅致的地毯。

original: "I'm a retiree. I've got plenty of time to decorate," he explains, before sitting into a well-cushioned armchair, oofing all the way.
translation: 「我退休了。有的是时间装修，」他解释道，然后坐进一张软垫扶手椅，一路往下陷。

original: "So... you trained my mother," you say, keeping on your feet, full of determination, even as he gestures for you to sit down. "Now I want you to train me," you reaffirm, and he nods.
translation: 「所以……你训练过我母亲，」你说道，站着没动，尽管他示意你坐下，但你依然充满决心。「现在我要你训练我，」你再次确认，他点了点头。

original: "What for?" he asks, peering over his steeped fingers, "What are you hoping to accomplish?"
translation: 「为了什么？」他问道，双手交叉放在膝盖上，目光透过指尖看向你，「你希望达成什么目标？」

original: You close your eyes. It's been so long. You were just a child, then... but not now. "To defeat who even my mother could not," you say, will unwavering, unlike your voice.
translation: 你闭上眼睛。已经过去太久了。那时你还只是个孩子……但现在不是了。「为了打倒连我母亲都无法战胜的对手。」你说，意志坚定，尽管声音有些颤抖。

original: "The Dragon God..." he says, in disbelieving tones, sitting forward in his chair.
translation: 「龙神……」他用难以置信的语气说道，身体在椅子上前倾。

original: "No, the Demon King."
translation: 「不，是魔王。」

original: "Right, the Demon King..." he says, in disbelieving tones, still sitting forward in his chair. "That's... not gonna lie, that's not happening. Still, though, we all have dreams," he says, sitting back in his chair, arms on the armrests, relaxed. "Worth a shot."
translation: 「对，魔王……」他依然用难以置信的语气说道，仍然前倾着身子。「这……说实话，不可能做到。不过嘛，我们都有梦想。」他向后靠在椅子上，手臂搭在扶手上，放松下来。「值得一试。」

original: As reassuring as his words are, you feel a hint of trepidation. You finally take his offer to sit, and he leans forward again to pour you some tea. You take the floral cup he offers you and sip at it... it's long cold. The fire is nearly extinguished.
translation: 尽管他的话令人安心，你还是感到一丝忐忑。你终于接受了他的邀请坐下，他再次前倾身子为你倒了些茶。你接过他递来的印花茶杯啜了一口……茶早就凉了。炉火也快要熄灭了。

original: "So you'll train me, then?" you ask, and he nods, "Like you trained my mother?"
translation: 「那么你会训练我吗？」你问道，他点了点头，「就像你训练我母亲那样？」

original: "I... wasn't your mother's trainer, per se. I was -a- trainer, and she purchased my services once or twice... once..." he says, trailing off, and you feel your heart sink. "B-but that still counts, right? Maybe without my help, she never would have killed the Moon Bug."
translation: 「我……严格来说，并不是你母亲的训练师。我只是—一个训练师，她买过我一两次服务……一次……」他越说声音越小，你感到心头一沉。「但—但那也算数吧？也许没有我的帮助，她根本杀不了月虫。」

original: "Moon Lord."
translation: 「月主。」

original: He ignores your correction. "If I can help her kill the Worm Lord, I can help you kill the Demon King, theoretically. Not a doubt in my mind," he says, although it's plain on his face that he's doubting even the basic premise of what he's saying, even putting aside what he said earlier.
translation: 他无视了你的纠正。「如果我能帮她杀死虫主，理论上我也能帮你杀死魔王。我毫不怀疑，」他说，尽管他脸上明显在怀疑自己所说的基本前提，甚至抛开他之前说过的话也不信。

original: "You'll help me? Even though I can't even pay you?" you say, gratefully, and he clicks his tongue.
translation: 「你会帮我？即使我连钱都付不起？」你感激地说，他啧了一声。

original: "Don't have to rub it in. Yeah, I'll help you. Right now. Clock's ticking," he says, pointing to his clockpiece standing against the wall, which is... clearly broken.
translation: 「不用一直强调。是的，我会帮你。就现在。时间不等人，」他说，指着靠墙立着的钟表……明显坏了。

original: "Do you really think, if I start now, I could... maybe, one day... defeat the Demon King?" you ask, trying to cling to hope.
translation: 「你真的觉得，如果我现在开始，我能……也许，有一天……打败魔王吗？」你问，试图抓住希望。

original: "Let me think... 'she slew a great demon in a faraway land. What became of her afterwards, few know, not even her own son' - that's you, right? Or do I have the wrong Dune Fjord slaying mama?" he says, apparently having finally recalled something about your mother and her story.
translation: 「让我想想……‘她在远方杀死了一只大恶魔。之后她怎么样了，很少有人知道，连她自己的儿子都不知道’—那就是你，对吧？还是我记错了杀沙丘峡魔的妈妈？」他说，显然终于想起了你母亲和她故事的某件事。

original: You politely ignore his error and focus on what he's implying. "My mother was great and powerful. I'm... me," you say, dejected, and he stands up - you're so surprised, you stand up as well, and he grasps your shoulders.
translation: 你礼貌地忽略了他的错误，专注于他暗示的意思。「我母亲伟大而强大。而我……就是我，」你沮丧地说，他站了起来—你太惊讶了，也站了起来，他抓住了你的肩膀。

original: "Well, even she had to start somewhere. She didn't simply take up the holy sword Irathel and march off to defeat the Moon Lord half-cocked. She had to stab thousands of inconsequential slimes and fauna before that," he says, reassuring you.
translation: 「嗯，就算她也得从某个地方开始。她可不是随随便便拿起圣剑伊拉瑟尔就跑去打败月之王的。在那之前，她得先戳死几千只无关紧要的史莱姆和野生动物。」他安慰你说。

original: "My mother didn't take up the holy sword Irathel at all. That was her cohort, the Paladin of Drepnir," you say, rapidly running out of patience.
translation: 「我妈妈根本没拿起过圣剑伊拉瑟尔。那是她的同伴，德雷普尼尔圣骑士干的。」你说，很快就不耐烦了。

original: "Holy sword, magic bracelet, whatever. The point is that her magical items dropped off of some less powerful creatures, and the items she used to defeat those schlubs dropped off of even less powerful creatures, maybe even people. In a world where the only way to get more powerful is to kill and steal, you have to start small," he says, his eyes full of kindness.
translation: 「圣剑也好，魔法手镯也罢，什么都行。重点是她的魔法物品是从一些弱小的生物身上掉落的，而她用来打败那些废物的装备则是从更弱小的生物甚至人身上掉落的。在这个变强的唯一方法就是杀戮和偷窃的世界里，你得从小处开始。」他说，眼中满是善意。

original: "I don't know if I have it in me," you say, hesitance gnawing at you.
translation: 「我不知道自己有没有那个本事。」你说，犹豫啃噬着你。

original: "That's horse manure," he says, slapping your shoulder, painfully, "If your mother could slay the Goon Lord, you can slay the Demon King. Uh, 50/50 odds, at least. Probably," he says, waving his hand about noncommittally.
translation: 「那是放屁。」他说着用力拍了下你的肩膀，痛得要命，「要是你妈能宰掉那个傻逼领主，你就能干掉魔王。呃，至少五五开吧。大概。」他漫不经心地挥着手说。

original: "You think so?" you ask, and he... admittedly takes a second to answer.
translation: 「你真这么想？」你问，而他……承认他花了一秒钟才回答。

original: "You're your mother's son. I'm sure you can take a life like the best of 'em. And if you can't, a few hours bashing rats with a stick will get you up to speed," he says, confidently. "But first things first. You'll need to pick a class."
translation: 「你是你妈的儿子。我保证你能像他们中最厉害的那样夺走生命。要是你做不到，花几个小时拿棍子敲老鼠就能让你赶上来。」他自信地说，「但首先第一件事。你得选个职业。」

original: You blink. "A... class? Like, the remedial kind, or...?"
translation: 你眨了眨眼。「一个……阶级？像是补课之类的，还是……？」

original: "No, no," he says, waving his hand dismissively, "a hero class. You know, what defines you as an adventurer. It represents a lifetime of training, discipline, and learning, as well as your personal outlook, philosophy, and primary strategy."
translation: 「不，不，」他摆摆手说，「是英雄职业。你知道，就是定义你作为冒险者的东西。它代表了你一生的训练、纪律和学习，以及你的个人观点、哲学和主要策略。」

original: You nod along. "Uh... then... shouldn't I have selected my class, like, a long time ago?"
translation: 你跟着点头。「呃……那么……我是不是应该很久以前就选好职业了？」

original: "You'd think, right? Anyway, just pick one. This is important, and you shouldn't be reckless in choosing, but... I skipped lunch, and it's getting late, so, you know..." he says, waving his hand along.
translation: 「按理说是这样，对吧？总之，随便选一个吧。这事很重要，你选的时候不能太随意，但是……我错过午饭了，而且时间不早了，所以，你懂的……」他边说边挥手。

original: "I don't... I don't even know what the options are," you say, already losing your nerve.
translation: 「我都不……我连有哪些选择都不知道，」你说，已经开始失去勇气了。

original: "Oh, for the love of... alright, here I am, your glorified class selection menu. Let's move this along. Ready? Here we go", he says, holding his hands up in the approximation of a rectangle.
translation: 「哦，看在……好吧，来，我就是你那个花哨的职业选择菜单。咱们快点搞定。准备好了吗？开始了，」他说着，双手比划出一个近似长方形的形状。

original: "WARRIOR", he says, out loud, before pausing for effect.
translation: 「战士，」他大声说道，然后停顿了一下以制造效果。

original: "Warriors are martial adepts, boasting excellent fighting skills and physical fitness. This is adventurer classic, but the classics are classics for a reason."
translation: 「战士是武术大师，拥有出色的战斗技巧和身体素质。这是冒险者的经典职业，但经典之所以是经典是有原因的。」

original: "Awesome! Mom was really strong. I like it," you say, and he scratches his chin, trying to avoid eye contact.
translation: 「太棒了！妈妈真的很强。我喜欢这个，」你说着，他挠了挠下巴，试图避开眼神接触。

original: "Well, that's true, but... it doesn't really suit you."
translation: 「嗯，这倒没错，但是……这个职业不太适合你。」

original: "Why do you say that?"
translation: 「为什么这么说？」

original: "Well... who's going to believe that someone that looks like you has a high STR score?"
translation: 「呃……谁会相信像你这样的人有高STR值？」

original: "That's..."
"And besides, everyone picks Warrior. It's like... vanilla ice cream. You don't want to be boring, do you? Even if your mother was one, you've got to forge your own path."
translation: 「而且，每个人都选战士。这就像……香草冰淇淋。你不想太无聊了吧？就算你妈妈是战士，你也得走出自己的路。」

original: "I... I guess you're right."
translation: 「我……我想你说得对。」

original: "Alright, next. ROGUE." he says, again, loudly, leaving an awkward silence lingering for a moment.
translation: 「好了，下一个。盗贼。」他又大声说道，留下一阵尴尬的沉默。

original: "Rogues are highly skilled, lightly armored combatants. They represent the cream of the crop in terms of their agility. They're masters of outmaneuvering their opponents and avoiding potentially fatal blows. Pretty nice, huh?"
translation: 「盗贼是技术高超、轻甲作战的单位。他们代表了敏捷中的佼佼者。他们是战术迂回和躲避致命一击的大师。很不错，对吧？」

original: "Yeah! I could use two weapons, and sneak behind enemies, and kill them before they even see me! I wouldn't even have to be a good fighter."
translation: 「是啊！我可以用两把武器，潜行到敌人背后，在他们看见我之前就干掉他们！我甚至不需要是个好战士。」

original: "True, true... but you'd also have a bad reputation. Would your mother really be proud if her son became a world class, back-stabbing murderer?"
translation: 「没错，没错……但你也会名声很差。如果你儿子成了世界级的背后捅刀子的杀人犯，你母亲真的会骄傲吗？」

original: "...no, I guess not. Fine. Next."
translation: 「……不，我想不会。好吧。下一个。」

original: "PALADIN. Formidable, stalwart, and holier-than-thee..."
translation: 「圣骑士。强大、坚定、比你更神圣……」

original: "Thou."
"Pardon?"
translation: 「什么？」

original: "It's 'Holier-than-thou.'"
translation: 是「比你更神圣」。

original: "That doesn't seem right. Really?"
translation: 「感觉不对吧？真的？」

original: "I'm pretty sure. If we go to the library, we could look it up."
translation: 「我很确定。如果去图书馆的话，我们可以查一查。」

original: "I mean, yeah, but... do you want to go all the way to the library just to see how people used to talk?" he says, implying that it's a giant waste of time.
translation: 「我是说，行吧……但你真的想大老远跑去图书馆，就为了看看古人怎么说话？」他说道，暗示这完全是浪费时间。

original: "It just sounds weird, is all," you say.
translation: 「就是听着怪怪的，仅此而已。」你说。

original: "ANYWAY, Paladin..."
translation: 「不管怎样，圣骑士……」

original: "Wouldn't the plural be 'Paladins'?"
translation: 「复数形式不是'圣骑士们'吗？」

original: "PALADINS are resilient and heavily armored, and are capable of holy magicks. Are you going to say something about how I spelled 'magick'?"
translation: 「圣骑士坚韧且重甲，能够使用神圣魔法。你是不是想说我拼错了'magick'这个单词？」

original: "No... that... speaks for itself."
translation: 「不……那个……它自己已经说明了一切。」

original: "...I'll give you a sovereign if you'll shut up and let me describe the Paladin."
translation: 「……我给你一个金币，只要你闭嘴让我描述圣骑士。」

original: "Done."
translation: 「成交。」

original: *RECEIVED 1 GOLD SOVEREIGN*
translation: *收到1枚金币*

original: "...and so, because of this, they're the most defensive class. For some reason, there's no playable Cleric or Healer class, so they're the only healers. They adhere to a strict code of ethics, and are sworn in by vows to uphold law, no matter the circumstance. Are you prepared to take those vows?"
translation: 「……所以，正因为如此，他们是最具防御性的职业。不知为何，没有可玩的牧师或治疗师职业，所以他们成了唯一的治疗者。他们遵守严格的道德准则，并发誓在任何情况下都维护法律。你准备好接受这些誓言了吗？」

original: "I'm more of a descriptivist myself, really."
translation: 「其实我自己更倾向于描述主义。」

original: "RANGER. As their name suggests, they're unparalleled at ranged combat. I could actually see you with a bow." he says, rubbing his chin under his beard.
translation: 「游侠。顾名思义，他们在远程战斗方面无可匹敌。我确实觉得你适合用弓。」他摸着胡须下的下巴说道。

original: "What's that supposed to mean?"
translation: 「这话什么意思？」

original: "Nothing, you just seem like the type that would use a bow."
translation: 「没什么，只是你看起来像会用弓的那种类型。」

original: "Because you're... uh..."
translation: 「因为你是……呃……」

original: "You know what? Never mind. I don't want to be a Ranger anyway. Mom never used a bow."
translation: 「你知道吗？算了。反正我也不想当游侠。妈妈从来没使过弓。」

original: "Right. MAGE. Mages wield the very nature of magic itself against their foes, and their power is legend, but their actual combat ability is without peer. Because nobody's as bad at fighting as a mage."
translation: 「没错。法师。法师运用魔法本身的力量对抗敌人，他们的力量堪称传说，但实际战斗能力却无人能及—因为没有人比法师更不擅长战斗了。」

original: "That's not like mom at all. I think I'll pass, phenomenal cosmic power or no."
translation: 「那跟老妈完全不像。我想还是算了，管它什么惊世骇俗的宇宙力量。」

original: "That's probably a good call. They're more of a party class, anyway."
translation: 「那大概是个明智的选择。反正他们更适合组队当个辅助职业。」

original: You sigh. You wonder just how many classes there are... and if it will really make a difference if none of them suit you. Maybe you should just be a warrior and try to make it work...
translation: 你叹了口气。你纳闷到底有多少职业……以及如果没有一个适合你，那是否真的能有什么改变。也许你干脆就当个战士，凑合着试试看……

original: He doesn't say anything for a bit. He looks like he's thinking... about what, you're not sure.
translation: 他沉默了一会儿。看起来他在思考……想些什么，你不太确定。

original: "...ENCHANT...ER. Enchanters... can do just about everything. High-level Enchanters are among the most powerful and revered of all adventurers. Their stats are balanced, they can wear most equipment, and they can use both light and dark magicks. One of your mom's closest allies was an Enchanter, even."
translation: 「……魔法剑……士。魔法剑士……几乎什么都能做。高阶魔法剑士是所有冒险者中最强大、最受尊敬的一类。他们的属性均衡，能装备大多数武器防具，还能同时使用光明与黑暗魔法。就连你老妈最亲密的盟友中，也有一位是魔法剑士呢。」

original: "...that sounds great. Alright, I'll be an Enchanter."
translation: 「……听起来很不错。好吧，那我就当魔法剑士了。」

original: "Just like that? You don't want to look at their st..."
translation: 「就这样？你不想看看他们的属……」

original: [You are now an Enchantress] [Level 1]
translation: [你现在是女巫] [等级 1]

original: "Alright! Class selected. What next?"
translation: 「好了！职业选定了。接下来呢？」

original: "Well, uh... you should get familiar with your character information screen... uhm..."
translation: 「呃，嗯……你该熟悉一下你的角色信息界面……嗯……」

original: "Why are all of my stats so... low?"
translation: 「为什么我的所有属性都这么……低？」

original: "They're... balanced."
translation: 「它们……很平衡。」

original: "Wait, my Charisma is 7?! What does that even do?"
translation: 「等等，我的魅力是7？！那有什么用？」

original: "Nothing, far as I can tell. It's just, each class has one high stat. Someone had to get the useless one."
translation: 「没什么，据我所知。只是每个职业都有一个高属性。总要有人拿到那个没用的。」

original: "You said Enchanters were really good!"
translation: 「你说过魔法剑士很厉害！」

original: "I said 'high-level' Enchanters are among the most powerful and revered. Level 1 Enchanters are worse than worthless; nobody takes them seriously. That's part of the reason why the high-level ones are respected - it takes a special individual to make the crappy starting class into something worthwhile. Good luck."
translation: 「我说的是‘高级’魔法剑士是最强大最受尊敬的之一。一级魔法剑士比没用还差；没人把他们当回事。这也是为什么高级魔法剑士受人尊敬的原因之一—需要有个特别的人才能把这个破烂初始职业变得有价值。祝你好运。」

original: You feel... numb. Dumbfounded. Before you even know what's happening, you realize that he's led you to the door, where he then pushes you outside.
translation: 你感觉……麻木。目瞪口呆。还没反应过来，你就发现他把你领到了门口，然后把你推了出去。

original: "W-wait! What about my training?" you ask, defiantly.
translation: 「等、等等！我的训练呢？」你挑衅地问道。

original: "That was it. I'm just certified as a class-change trainer. Never got my skill training certification. Bad grades. One time, we snuck on top of the school and--"
translation: 「就这些。我只是认证了职业转换训练师。从来没拿到技能训练证书。成绩太差。有一次，我们偷偷爬上学校屋顶—」

original: "THAT'S IT? What am I supposed to do now?" you ask, sounding more petulant than you intended.
translation: 「就这？我现在该怎么办？」你问道，听起来比你预想的还要任性。

original: "Now? Now you're gonna want to get moving. Six days from now, the mountain pass out of this region is gonna be frozen over. Any time you spend dawdling here..."
translation: 「现在？现在你该出发了。六天后，这片区域的山隘就要结冻了。你在这儿磨蹭的时间……」

original: "You really can't help me?"
translation: 「你真的不能帮我吗？」

original: "Well... I can show you some basic swordplay, but that's it. Take the crash course, or we'll be here all day."
translation: 「呃……我可以教你一些基础剑术，但也仅此而已。要么上速成课，要么咱一整天都耗在这儿。」

original: "I think even a single day would still be a crash course..."
translation: 「我看就算只花一天，也算是速成课了……」

original: "Are you paying? No? Didn't think so. Fine, though, a day, max. Pick your poison."
translation: 「你付钱吗？不付吧？我猜也是。行吧，那就最多一天。选你的毒药吧。」

original: You decide against training. He shrugs.
translation: 你决定不训练。他耸了耸肩。

original: You decide to get some basic instruction, not wanting to waste too much time - either way, you're going to have to learn as you go. After a few hours of him showing you the ropes, you feel a little more confident, but only barely.
translation: 你决定接受一些基础指导—不想浪费太多时间—反正你都得边干边学。他带你上手了几个小时，你稍微有了点底气，但也仅仅是稍微。

original: He sighs as you insist on a full day of training. It's not an intensive course - he mostly just rattles off what comes to mind, shows you how to throw a cut, defend against various attacks using footwork, your shield, and your own weapon, and has you do a few drills.
translation: 他叹口气，因为你坚持要训练一整天。这不是什么强化的课程——他主要就是把自己想到的东西随便说说，教你如何挥砍、如何运用步法、你的盾牌和自身武器来防御各种攻击，还会让你做几个练习套路。

original: By the end of it, the sun has set and risen again, and you feel more confident... if only a little.
translation: 到结束时，太阳已经落下又升起，你感觉更有自信了……尽管只是稍微。

original: You don't feel particularly prepared, still. "What now?"
translation: 你仍然觉得自己没有完全准备好。「现在怎么办？」

original: "Now... well, first thing is getting supplies. Better visit the Shop in town. You'll know which one - it's the only one. And you'll need someone to teach you magic, since an Enchantress without magic is just asking for it, but it's magic, so I ain't gotta explain... look, my tea's getting cold. Run along now."
translation: 「现在……嗯，第一件事就是搞到补给。最好去镇上那家店看看。你知道是哪家——镇上就这一家。而且你得找人教你魔法，毕竟一个不会魔法的女术士就是找死，但这是魔法，所以我就不用多解释了……瞧，我的茶快凉了。快走吧。」

original: He slams the door shut. You feel... different. Certainly, you feel more in tune with the flowing of the air around you, and looking at your hands, there does seem to be a noticeable... change in your appearance as well. Supplies, he said... well, first thing's first. Although, you suppose, this is now the second thing.
translation: 他砰地关上了门。你感觉……有些不一样了。确实，你觉得自己与周围气流的流动更加协调，低头看看自己的双手，外貌上也出现了明显的……变化。补给品，他说道……嗯，先做要紧事吧。不过，你心想，现在这已经是第二件要紧事了。

original: As you walk away from the cottage towards the village proper, you start to wonder.
translation: 当你从小屋走向村庄内部时，你开始思索起来。

original: To slay the Demon King... will that really improve the lives of the people you care most about? You wonder. You can barely remember it, but you've heard stories of what life was like under the Moon Lord's thumb... assuming it had a thumb, anyway. The threat of the Demon King, by comparison, seems... remote.
translation: 杀死魔王……这真的能改善你最在乎的人们的生活吗？你思索着。你几乎记不清了，但你听过关于生活在月之领主统治下的故事……假设它确实有拇指的话。相比之下，魔王的威胁似乎……很遥远。

original: Well, your job as village layabout has come to an end, surely. If you can give back to all the people who helped and supported you, then that's just what you're going to do.
translation: 嗯，你作为村庄游手好闲者的日子肯定已经结束了。如果能为所有帮助和支持过你的人做出回报，那就是你接下来要做的事。

original: First, you set out for the village itself - knowing that the road to the Demon King's fortress, far in the northwest, beyond the mountains, will be daunting, you take your first true steps in that direction - to your first home, Nadir, and then beyond.
translation: 首先，你朝村庄本身出发—知道通往西北远方山外魔王要塞的路途会很艰难，你朝着那个方向迈出了真正的第一步—前往你的第一个家，纳迪尔，然后继续向前。

original: You return to the cottage, but find that he's no longer there.
translation: 你回到小屋，但发现他已经不在了。

original: You wait around for a while, but he doesn't return - maybe he's out on the town, or foraging for squirrels to eat.
translation: 你等了一会儿，但他没有回来—也许他去了镇上，或者去找松鼠吃了。

original: You leave, disappointed.
translation: 你失望地离开了。

original: You return to the cottage, and the trainer is there, seemingly waiting for you...
translation: 你回到小屋，训练师在那里，似乎在等你……

original: "The legendary... ice cream. With its secrets, I can bring out your true potential," he says, sensing the ice cream in your pouch. You take it out and he holds out his hand for you to give it to him.
translation: 「传说中的……冰淇淋。有了它的秘密，我就能激发出你的真正潜力，」他说道，感知到了你口袋里的冰淇淋。你把它拿出来，他伸出手，示意你交给他。

original: "Really?"
translation: “真的？”

original: "Yeah," he says, licking the ice cream. "This is so good. Anyway, now you know how to use Blitz stance. Go nuts."
translation: “是啊，”他舔着冰淇淋说道。“真好吃。不管怎样，现在你知道怎么用闪击姿态了。去大干一场吧。”

original: You enter the village of Nadir proper - the main street stretches before you, and you know the shop isn't far. On your way to it, you happen across a trio of old, crusty men telling stories around a bonfire, although one of them doesn't say a word at all. You walk past, nonchalantly, but listen in on their conversation, hiding behind a nearby retaining wall.
translation: 你终于走进了纳迪尔村庄—主街在你面前延伸，你知道商店就在不远处。前往商店的路上，你偶然遇到三个又老又脏的家伙围坐在篝火旁讲故事，不过其中一个一句话也没说。你若无其事地走过，但躲在附近的挡土墙后面偷听他们的对话。

original: Old Man A
translation: 老头甲

original: "Did ya hear about that adventurer boy what wandered into the orc camp down by Nagle's Crook?" one man asks, although it's obvious from the reaction of the other two that they don't much know or care about it.
translation: “你们听说那个冒险者小子跑进内格尔弯附近的兽人营地的事了吗？”一个男人问道，不过从另外两人的反应来看，他们对此不太了解，也不太关心。

original: Old Man B
translation: 老头乙

original: "Nah. They kill 'em?"
translation: “没。他们杀了他？”

original: "Pahh, that'd be a borin' story, wouldnit? Mighta been better for him, though. Daft orcs couldn't tell 'em from a lass, so they blasted his ass six ways to sundown before he escaped. Got back to town with enough orc jism up his butt to qualify as a caravan hisself!"
translation: 「呸，那故事可没意思了，对吧？不过对他本人来说可能还好点。那些傻兽人分不清他是男是女，在他逃跑前把他屁股操了个底朝天。等他回镇子的时候，屁股里塞的兽人精液够他自个儿当一支商队了！」

original: The man threw back his head in laughter, and his compatriot snickered, a grin visible beneath his mangy beard.
translation: 老人仰头大笑，他的同伴也咯咯地笑了起来，脏兮兮的胡子下面露出一个嬉笑的表情。

original: Old Man C
translation: 老人丙

original: "Ya think that's funny? A fresh-faced lad said he'd slay that ogre prince fella making trouble in the lowlands, and bring 'is head to the princess in exchange for her hand in joyous marriage 'n all that."
translation: 「你觉得那很好笑？有个鲜嫩的小伙子说他要去杀掉那个在低地闹事的食人魔王子，把他的脑袋带给公主，换她答应和他喜结良缘什么的。」

original: He shakes his head, fighting back a fit of giggling. Then he leans over and spits into a spittoon.
translation: 他摇了摇头，忍住了想笑的冲动。然后他俯身朝痰盂里啐了一口。

original: "Next time anyone saw 'im, he was dressed much the same as the princess whose royal pussy he'd been chasin' after - and the ogre prince was slippin' him a fat one under the skirts and up his arse. Ever seen an ogre dick? Me neither, but I reckon that boy ain't a happy wife."
translation: 「再有人见到他的时候，他穿得就跟那个他一直在追公主的皇家婊子差不多—而那食人魔王子正往他裙子底下塞进一根粗大的玩意儿，直捣他的后穴。见过食人魔的阳具吗？我没见过，不过我猜那小子肯定当不上一个快乐的新娘。」

original: You feel a sinking feeling in your stomach. Does that sort of thing really happen? It can't be. These old, dirty men must be telling tall bawdy tales just to pass the time. There's nothing more to it than that.
translation: 你感到胃里一阵翻腾。这种事真的会发生吗？不可能。这些肮脏的老东西一定是在讲些下流的黄色笑话打发时间。仅此而已。

original: "I can do ya one better. Hear about those shebitches that caught licker... lycra... the wolf sickness, y'know what I mean," he says, and you're not sure if the drunkenness has addled his brain or if it came pre-addled.
translation: 「我能给你讲个更绝的。听说了吗，那些母狗染上了舔……莱卡……狼病，你懂我意思吧，」他说，你不确定是醉酒让他脑子糊涂了，还是他本来就这样。

original: "Yeah, what about 'em?"
translation: 「啊？她们咋了？」

original: "Well, rumor 'as it they've got between their legs what an alpha male wolf's got between 'is. An' they like to pounce on unsuspecting men and give 'em what for," he says, pantomiming with his hands what everyone listening could picture in their heads.
translation: 「嗯，据说是这样—她们两条腿中间长着公狼头狼胯下那玩意儿。还喜欢扑倒毫无防备的男人，狠狠教训他们一顿，」他边说边用手比划着，在场每个人脑子里都能想象出那画面。

original: "Yah, so? How does that compare to an ogre's fat one?"
translation: 「是吗？那跟食人魔的大鸡巴比咋样？」

original: "Well, y'see," he replies, leaning in, conspiratorially, but stage-whispering loud enough that you can still easily hear, "when a dog is givin' another dog the business, they don't wan' 'em to leave before the business is concluded, y'see. So it swells up, big as a dogfruit... an' I just got the name," he says, wiping his mouth in disgust."
translation: 「哎呀，你看，」他凑近身子，一副密谋的样子，却又压低嗓音大声说道，让你能听得清清楚楚，「公狗跟母狗办事的时候，可不想让母狗在完事之前跑了，你懂吧。所以那玩意儿会胀起来，大得跟狗卵子似的……我这才刚想起这词儿，」他厌恶地擦了擦嘴。

original: "Big as a dogfruit? Oof... tha' must be 'n ordeal."
translation: 「大得跟狗卵子似的？唔……那肯定得遭老罪了。」

original: "Yah, let me tell ya, from one man to another, if a she bitch is lookin' frisky and ye can't fight her off, ye best off swallowin' yer tongue."
translation: 「可不是嘛，我跟你说，男人跟男人讲真心话，要是母狗发情了，你又打不过她，最好还是咬舌自尽算了。」

original: You back away from the two men, who seem awfully amused with themselves, and check that your training sword is where you left it. It sounds like you'll be needing it to be sharp and at the ready, because the world is a dangerous... uh... the image of a noble knight swearing to slay the ogre prince flashes in your mind, and then...
translation: 你从两个男人身边退开，他们似乎对自己感到极其好笑，然后检查你的训练剑是否还在原处。听起来你得把它磨锋利并随时准备好，因为这个世界很危险……呃……一位高贵的骑士发誓要杀死食人魔王子的画面闪过你的脑海，然后……

original: You shake your head. The shop is just down this road. You follow it, and spot the shop marked, well, 'Shop'.
translation: 你摇了摇头。商店就在这条路上。你顺着路走，看到了那个店铺，上面标着，嗯，「店铺」。

original: When you enter, you see that the shop has fruit laid out in baskets on tables and on counters, more fruit than anyone has ever had need for. Since you get your food courtesy of the township, you've never had reason to step inside.
translation: 进去后，你看到店里的桌子上、柜台上都摆着篮子，里面放着水果，水果多到任何人都用不完。由于你的食物由镇子提供，你从来没有理由进来过。

original: Behind the counter, you see a fresh-faced girl who you've seen around town, but never spoken to.
translation: 柜台后面，你看到一个面生的女孩，你在镇上见过她，但从未说过话。

original: "Hey there, what can I get for ya?" she says, putting a basket full of apples down on the shop counter.
translation: 「嘿，你好，要点什么？」她说着，把一个装满苹果的篮子放在柜台上。

original: You can't quite place her accent. She hasn't looked over at you yet responding only to the sound of the shop door shutting behind you.
translation: 你不太能分辨她的口音。她还没有抬头看你，只是回应着你身后店门关上的声音。

original: "Oh, you're new around here, aren't you?" she asks, once she turns and sees your face.
translation: 「哦，你是新来的，对吧？」她转过身看到你的脸时问道。

original: "I've... lived here my whole life..." you say, but she just smiles politely in response. She wipes her hands - she'd clearly just been working in the apple grotto - and clears her throat.
translation: 「我……一辈子都住在这里……」你说道，但她只是礼貌地笑了笑作为回应。她擦了擦手—显然刚才一直在苹果园里干活—然后清了清嗓子。

original: "Name's Shopkeep. You can call me Shopkeep," she says, full of energy. "What would you like to buy? Oh, and this cute thing isn't for sale," she says, gesturing to herself.
translation: 「我叫店主。你可以叫我店主，」她充满活力地说道。「你想买点什么？哦，这件可爱的东西可不卖，」她指了指自己。

original: "I... didn't think that..." you say, flustered, and she looks down at her dress and pulls on the hem, lifting it slightly.
translation: 「我……没这么想……」你慌乱地说，而她却低头看向自己的裙子，拽了拽裙摆，微微提了起来。

original: "I made it myself. It's my favorite dress. Also my only one," she says, dropping the hem and shrugging. "So, what'll it be?"
translation: 「我自己做的。这是我最喜欢的裙子。也是我唯一的一条，」她放下裙摆，耸了耸肩。「所以，想买什么？」

original: You wipe your brow. Her dress, obviously. It's your turn to clear your throat. "Hrm... what do you sell?"
translation: 你擦了擦额头。她的裙子，这还用说。轮到你清嗓子了。「嗯……你都卖什么？」

original: "Oh, the usual," she says, although you're not sure what a 'shop' usually sells, "apples, pomegranates, dogfruit, and other seasonal fruit and spices. Wait, are you an adventurer?", she asks, eyes suddenly wide with excitement. At the mention of 'dogfruit', your sphincter tightens.
translation: 「哦，就是些常见的东西，」她说道，虽然你并不确定「商店」通常都卖什么，「苹果、石榴、狗果，还有其他时令水果和香料。等等，你是冒险者吗？」她问道，眼睛突然兴奋地睁大了。一听到「狗果」这个词，你的括约肌就收紧了。

original: You feel a swelling of pride. "Why, yes, yes I am," you say, confidently.
translation: 你感到一阵自豪涌上心头。「哎呀，没错，我就是，」你自信地说道。

original: "Well then. I sell swords, knives, axes, hammers, maces, flails, guns, bombs... did I say knives? Knives," she says, smiling sweetly, even as she kicks the display behind her, which flips around to reveal... well, exactly what she'd said. An arsenal.
translation: 「那么。我卖剑、刀、斧、锤、钉头锤、连枷、枪、炸弹……我说过刀吗？刀，」她说着，甜甜一笑，同时踢了踢身后的展示架，架子翻转过来……露出她刚说的那些东西。一座兵器库。

original: Your mouth hangs agape.
translation: 你目瞪口呆。

original: "Oh, bows, also," she says, sensing your sudden anxiety, looking like she just remembered something, pointing over her shoulder at an array of short and longbows.
translation: 「哦，还有弓，」她察觉到你的突然紧张，好像刚想起什么似的，指了指身后一排短弓和长弓。

original: "No, no, I don't know how to... did you say bombs? Uh... what kind of... swords do you have?" you ask, trying to swallow your surprise.
translation: 「不，不，我不会……你刚说炸弹？呃……你有哪些……剑？」你问道，努力压下惊讶。

original: "All kinds - longswords, shortswords, broadswords, bastardswords, single-edged, which are just knives, really, double-edged, good to wear gauntlets with those, falchions, scimitars, rapiers, mediumswords, machetes..." she continues, listing them off without taking a breath.
translation: 「各种—长剑、短剑、阔剑、混种剑、单刃剑，其实也就是刀啦，双刃剑，适合戴护手甲用的，弯刃刀、弯刀、细剑、中剑、砍刀……」她一口气数下去，都不带喘气的。

original: "I think... uh... I think a longsword is fine."
translation: 「我觉得……呃……长剑就行。」

original: Her eyes light up. "What kind?"
translation: 她眼睛一亮。「哪种？」

original: "A pointy one, please," you say - you don't know the first thing about swords.
translation: “请给我一把尖的，”你说—你对剑一窍不通。

original: "Got ya!" she says, turning on her heel to face the display of weapons... only to open a big iron chest, which you can see is actually filled to the brim with swords of all kinds - there must be dozens, thrown haphazardly inside. She dons a pair of thick gloves and then dives in.
translation: “明白！”她说着，转过身面对武器展示架……却打开了一个大铁箱，你能看到里面塞满了各种各样的剑—至少有几十把，杂乱地扔在里面。她戴上一副厚手套，然后把手伸了进去。

original: You swallow, hard, as she searches deep in the chest...
translation: 你用力咽了口唾沫，看着她往箱子里深处翻找……

original: ...paying little mind to the fact that she's bending fully at the waist, and her skirt is not long enough to hide her panty-clad rear, which she wiggles at you in her search, either oblivious or uncaring.
translation: ……她几乎没注意到自己完全弯下了腰，裙子不够长，遮不住她穿内裤的臀部，她在翻找时冲你扭着屁股，不是没注意到就是毫不在意。

original: You wonder if she didn't measure her height properly, or if her rear wasn't quite so large when she sewed the dress... but it's hard to really think through hypothetical scenarios.
translation: 你想知道是不是她量身高没量准，或者缝这条裙子时她的屁股还没这么大……但真假难辨的假设实在让人难以细想。

original: The fabric stretches thin over her admirably-sized bottom.
translation: 布料在她尺寸可观的下半身上绷得薄薄的。

original: She struggles to lift something heavy, and you can't look away.
translation: 她费力地抬起什么重物，你目光无法移开。

original: She rifles around while picking things up, and you're mesmerized.
translation: 她四处翻找着捡起东西，你看得入了迷。

original: "I'll be right with ya," she says, and you clear your throat.
translation: 「我马上就来陪你，」她说着，你清了清嗓子。

original: You try not to gawk anymore, and soon, she stands back up with her arms full of blades.
translation: 你努力不再傻盯着看，很快，她就抱着一怀抱的刀剑直起身来。

original: "These are all pointy, good for stabbing and eviscerating, and more importantly, they're nice and light, so even weaklings can use them," she says, chipper as ever. She doesn't seem to mean any insult by it, so it seems like it's just an honest appraisal of your abilities. Which is... more insulting, probably.
translation: 「这些都很尖，适合捅刺和开膛，更重要的是，它们又轻又好使，连弱鸡都能用，」她一如既往地快活地说。她似乎并没有要侮辱你的意思，所以这大概只是对你能力的诚实评价。这……可能更侮辱人了。

original: "T-thanks... I guess I'll pick one..."
translation: 「谢、谢谢……我想我挑一把吧……」

original: She drops the lot of them down on the counter - that can't be safe, or a good way to handle the merchandise - and you carefully peruse them. There's a thin sword, clearly designed for thrusting, one that's a bit thicker, for thrusting or swinging it around, a thick one with a single edge, and a thick, double-edged one.
translation: 她把那一堆刀剑哐当一声扔在柜台上—这肯定不安全，也不是对待商品的正确方式—然后你仔细端详起来。有一把细剑，显然是设计用来刺击的；一把稍微厚点的，可以刺也可以挥砍；一把厚实的单刃刀；还有一把厚重的双刃剑。

original: When you finish making your selection, you hold it out from your body, feeling more confident than you've ever felt - but you still have no idea how to use it. Being Level 1 sucks.
translation: 当你挑好之后，你把它从身前举起来，感觉前所未有的自信—但你还是不知道该怎么用。等级1真是太操蛋了。

original: "Oh, no, not like that," she says, rounding the counter, "here, let me show you."
translation: 「哦，不，不是那样拿的，」她说着绕出柜台，「来，我给你示范一下。」

original: She stands behind you and grabs the sword, and you feel a lump in your throat. Her soft form presses against your back, and your heart races.
translation: 她站到你身后，握住了剑柄，你感到喉咙一阵发紧。她柔软的身躯贴在你背上，你的心跳骤然加速。

original: "Oh my... you're rather cute, aren't you? A shame you're going on a journey..." she says, and you feel your heart beating in your chest... and a stirring in your loins that isn't nearly as innocent.
translation: 「哎呀……你还挺可爱的嘛？可惜你就要踏上旅程了……」她说，你感到心脏在胸腔里狂跳……还有胯下那一点不那么单纯的骚动。

original: Her hand rests on yours, and her fingers trace up towards your wrist, when suddenly, she stops.
translation: 她的手覆在你的手上，指尖沿着向上滑到你的手腕，突然，她停住了。

original: "Well, I'm sure you know how to use it," she says, and you swallow, as she rounds the counter again, her skirt trailing behind her. "Before you go, have this - on the house."
translation: 「好吧，我肯定你知道怎么用它，」她说，你咽了口唾沫，看着她再次绕回柜台，裙摆拖在身后。「走之前，拿着这个—本店请客。」

original: She reaches under the counter, procuring a large, oddly shaped bottle containing a bright red liquid, before handing it over to you. It's a... potion. It's bigger than any you've ever seen.
translation: 她伸手到柜台下面，拿出一个形状怪异的大瓶子，里面装着鲜红的液体，然后递给你。这是一瓶……药水。比你见过的任何药水都要大瓶。

original: She returns to her default position behind the counter and stares at you, smiling brightly, but silently. You start to turn away, and she finally speaks.
translation: 她回到了柜台后她常站的位置，看着你，灿烂地笑着，却一言不发。你正要转身离开，她终于开口了。

original: "Thanks for coming!" she says, waving to you as you leave, having procured the necessary - you hope - supplies.
translation: 「谢谢光临！」她说，在你离开时朝你挥手，你已备好了—但愿如此—所需的补给。

original: With weapon in hand, you set out. First stop - someone who will teach you magic. You could ask the townsfolk for a suggestion... but you have at least one option in mind: Meri, a magician your mother once traveled with, who now lives as a recluse a few days east, near the foot of the Burle mountains.
translation: 手持武器，你出发了。第一站—找个教你魔法的人。你可以向镇上的人打听建议……但你心里至少有一个选择：梅里，一位你母亲曾与之同行的魔法师，如今隐居在向东几天路程的地方，靠近布尔山地的山脚下。

original: You leave the town, feeling more full of gumption than you ever have before.
translation: 你离开小镇，感觉从未如此充满勇气。

original: "Yeah, yeah, keep suckin', keep suckin'..."
translation: 「对对，继续吸，继续吸……」

original: How did it end up like this?
translation: 怎么会落到这种地步？

original: "Nnnh, uuhnn... I'm gonna fuckin' nut!"
translation: 「嗯唔，唔嗯……老子要射了！」

original: You just wanted to be a hero... but you immediately got lost and...
translation: 你只想当个英雄……却立刻迷了路，然后……

original: "Take it right in your mouth!"
translation: 「直接含进嘴里！」

original: There'd been a... miscommunication. You had said that you were a hero, and that you weren't just going to... service any random, unwashed brigand that you blundered into, but... well, she explained that this was a toll road, and you had to pay the toll, no exceptions.
translation: 之前有那么一点……误会。你原来说过自己是个勇者，不会随便……伺候任何碰上的、不讲究的强盗，但……好吧，她解释说这是条收费道路，你必须付过路费，没有例外。

original: So you paid it.
translation: 于是你付了。

original: The thing rampaging in your mouth... you know what it is. You're not doing as much to get it soft as she is, pistoning it in and out of your face without a care in the world. The taste of her sweat and precum fills your mouth... and the way it throbs makes it clear that won't be all you taste.
translation: 在你嘴里横冲直撞的那玩意儿……你知道是什么。你并没有像她那样卖力地让它软下来，她把那东西在你的脸上毫无顾忌地来回抽送。她汗水和前精的味道充满了你的嘴……而那东西脉动的方式表明，你尝到的不止这些。

original: Your lips are barely on it when it finally does twitch... which puts it in a perfect position to make you taste it. Three long, sticky ropes blast out over your tongue before you can warn her that you're not a cum-gargling slut, even if it feels like you are.
translation: 当它终于抽搐时，你的嘴唇几乎还含在上面……这恰好让它能让你品尝个够。在你来得及警告她自己不是个含着精液漱口的婊子之前（尽管感觉上就是），三股又长又黏的液柱喷到你的舌头上。

original: It continues to fire all over inside, your cheeks, your teeth... it's... a bit of a mouthwash. You never had your mouth washed out with soap, but... now you've had your mouth washed out with semen. Your tongue is basted with it, and the flavour is unavoidable.
translation: 它继续在里面到处喷射，你的脸颊、你的牙齿……这……有点像漱口水。你从没用肥皂漱过口，但……现在你用精液漱了口。你的舌头上沾满了它，味道无法回避。

original: Toll Woman
translation: 收费女

original: "Thanks for the free blowie, "hero". Dunno what I woulda done with all that cocksnot in my goons if you didn't come along."
translation: 「谢啦，免费口交，『英雄』。要是你没来，我都不知道拿我那群傻蛋嘴里的那堆子子孙孙怎么办了。」

original: Her spermslime tasted about as good as could be expected... maybe being an adventurer isn't all it's cracked up to be. Or maybe... you're not really suited for it. You decide to retreat back to your hovel and make... something else of yourself, while trying to stifle a burp.
translation: 她的精液味道尝起来……跟预想的差不多。也许当冒险者根本不像传闻中那么美好……又或者……是你根本不适合干这行。你决定撤回自己的破窝，另谋……出路，同时努力憋住一个嗝。

original: It doesn't taste like regret, but... it's possible that regret is just saltier than you anticipated.
translation: 尝起来不像后悔的味道，不过……可能后悔本身就比你预想的要更咸一些。

original: If only you had gone the right way...
translation: 要是你走了正确的路就好了……

original: You manage to track down Meri's cottage - it being far on the opposite side of Nadir from your own humble hovel, you've never had an overwhelmingly compelling reason to come by here, and plenty of reason to stay away.
translation: 你设法找到了梅丽的小屋—它坐落在纳迪尔的另一端，离你自己那寒酸的小窝很远。你从来没有什么非要来这里的理由，反倒有无数理由躲得远远的。

original: Your curiosity about your mother had long since been overruled by your self-preservation, as Meri had a reputation for making boys like yourself... disappear. Whether it was something truly sinister or simply the murmurs of an ignorant peasantry jumping to conclusions about a local witch, you didn't want to chance it.
translation: 你对母亲的 curiosity 早就被自我保护的本能给压下去了，因为梅丽素有让像你这样的男孩……消失的名声。这背后究竟是真正的邪恶勾当，还是无知的村民对当地女巫妄加揣测的流言蜚语，你都不想去冒险求证。

original: Faced with few other options now, however, you decide to give the old witch a chance - and remind yourself not to call her that to her face.
translation: 然而如今，你面前的选择寥寥无几，你决定给这个老女巫一个机会—并在心里提醒自己，千万别当面这么叫她。

original: Her cottage is excessively plain - but there's an air of palpable... malice to it that you can't shake off. You warily approach the door, one hand on your sword's sheathe, the other balled in a fist against your chest, which thumps steadily and powerfully.
translation: 她的小屋过于朴素—但其中弥漫着一股你挥之不去的……恶意。你小心翼翼地靠近门口，一只手按在剑鞘上，另一只手握拳抵在胸前，心脏平稳而有力地跳动着。

original: You reach out said hand to knock, unwilling to remove your grip on your scabbard, and a hair's breadth away from the door, you hear a voice which stops you cold, as if frozen - "come in," it says, merrily, inviting.
translation: 你伸出那只手准备敲门，不愿松开抓着剑鞘的手，就在指尖距门板一发之隔时，一个声音让你瞬间僵住，仿佛被冻结—「进来吧，」它欢快而诱人地说道。

original: You drop your fingers down, slowly, carefully, to the door handle, and, after catching your breath, you push it open.
translation: 你缓缓地、小心地放下手指，搭在门把手上，深吸一口气后，推开了门。

original: The inside of the cottage smells... rather pleasant, actually, and it's much, much hotter than the autumn breeze outside. As the door closes behind you, in fact, you feel an overwhelming sensation of comfort and familiarity, even though the inside of the cottage is wholly unfamiliar to you.
translation: 小屋内部的气味……其实相当宜人，而且比外面的秋风要热得多。事实上，当门在你身后关上时，你感受到一种压倒性的舒适和熟悉感，尽管屋内的一切对你而言全然陌生。

original: It's a bit like you expect a witch's cottage to be - in that it surprises you entirely. The surfaces are immaculate, the fineries are quite fine, the upholstery looks pristine and expensive, deep blacks, oak browns, and fresh blood reds - it's the last thing you would expect on the outskirts of a town like Nadir.
translation: 这跟你预想的女巫小屋差不多—也就是说，它完全出乎你的意料。表面一尘不染，陈设相当精致，装饰物看起来崭新而昂贵，深邃的黑色、橡木棕色和鲜亮的血红色—这绝不是你在纳迪尔镇郊外会想到的东西。

original: There are, you eventually notice, some of the expected collections of a witch - tomes, less musty, but tomes nonetheless, and magic circles scrawled on the walls and floors, and great, beautiful crystals and orbs, each positioned on top of a holder, some large, some small, delicately enough to make you anxious.
translation: 你终于注意到，屋里有一些女巫常见的收藏—书籍，虽然没那么发霉，但毕竟是书卷，墙上和地板上潦草地画着魔法阵，还有巨大而美丽的晶体和球体，每个都放在一个底座上，有些大，有些小，摆放得精巧得让你不禁紧张。

original: And then, finally, you spot her, as a shadow darts across the floor and then seems to disappear beneath her garments. Even wearing a full-length robe, the curves of her body are visible and... otherworldly. No doubt the work of some foul magic.
translation: 接着，你终于看到了她—一道阴影掠过地板，然后似乎消失在她的衣袍之下。即使穿着全长法袍，她身体的曲线依然可见，而且……超凡脱俗。毫无疑问，是某种邪恶魔法的作用。

original: She turns to face you, looking away from a spherical crystal - or is it an orb? - a smile on her lips, and a hunger in her eyes. You smile politely at her and nod.
translation: 她转过身面向你，视线从一个球形水晶—或者说是个球体？—上移开，嘴角挂着微笑，眼中闪着饥渴的光芒。你礼貌地朝她笑了笑，点了点头。

original: You didn't expect her to be so... voluptuous. In fact, you had kind of expected her to be old and grizzled by now, like your mother's class trainer - instead, she doesn't look like she's aged a day since you were a child.
translation: 你没料到她竟然如此……丰腴。实际上，你本以为她现在已经年老色衰、满脸皱纹，就像你母亲的职业导师那样—可她却看起来跟你小时候见到时毫无变化。

original: Mage
translation: 法师

original: "My, my. Look who's all grown up.", she says, her voice deep, her presence large. Not exactly the slightness you would expect of a mage.
translation: “哎呀呀。看看谁已经长大了。”她说道，声音低沉，气场强大。并不像你预期中法师该有的那种纤弱感。

original: You find it difficult to respond - something about her presence is... intimidating. She has long, goldenrod hair, bright blue eyes, an impressive chest, and flaring hips that lead into long, lithe legs. She strikes a powerful figure, and the way her gaze fixes on you makes it near-impossible to return.
translation: 你发现很难回应—她身上有种……令人生畏的气势。她有一头长长的金黄花蕊色秀发，明亮的蓝眼睛，傲人的胸脯，以及向外展开的臀部，延伸出修长而柔韧的双腿。她展现出一个强大的形象，而她的目光锁定在你身上时，几乎让你无法回视。

original: "I remember you. My, you look just like your mother. Mmm... I love a man that looks anything but," she says, pumping her hips as she walks over to you, her heels striking a pleasant tone against her hard, wooden floor.
translation: “我记得你。哎呀，你长得真像你母亲。嗯……我就喜欢看起来完全不像男人的男人，”她说着，扭动臀部朝你走来，高跟鞋在硬木地板上敲出悦耳的声响。

original: She grabs you by the chin, and turns your head to inspect you. You finally work up the nerve to speak. "I'm an... enchanter now," you say, and she smiles politely, releasing your chin.
translation: 她抓住你的下巴，转动你的头仔细打量。你终于鼓起勇气开口。“我现在是……魔法剑士了，”你说，她礼貌地笑了笑，松开了你的下巴。

original: "Enchantress. But go on," she says, snapping her fingers, producing some phantasm of darkness that swirls around her fingertips like an ethereal bat, before she grabs hold of it and it vanishes in a cloud of nothingness.
translation: 「女术士。不过你继续说，」她说着，打了个响指，指尖萦绕着一团黑暗的幻影，如缥缈的蝙蝠般盘旋，随即她伸手一抓，幻影便化作虚无消失无踪。

original: "That's... that's why I'm here," you say, alluding to both your recent class change and the casual display she just put on, "I'm interested in magic."
translation: 「这……这就是我来这里的原因，」你说，暗指你最近的转职和她刚才随手展示的技艺，「我对魔法感兴趣。」

original: She giggles silently, bringing her hand lazily up her thigh, then pointing a long, sharp-nailed finger at you, flicking it upwards in a gentle arc. You feel an immense pressure pushing you back - your shoes slide along the floor, until your entire body is spread against her closed door, as if the pull of gravity has changed directions.
translation: 她无声地轻笑，慵懒地将手抬到大腿上，然后用一根长着尖利指甲的手指指向你，轻轻向上勾动。你感到一股巨大的压力将你向后推—你的鞋子在地板上滑动，直到整个身体被摊开在她紧闭的门上，仿佛重力的方向已经改变。

original: "Everyone is interested in magic," she says, "but not everyone gets to meet a magician. Aren't you interested in me?" she asks, curling her finger back around to gesture for you to come to her - a beckoning which you literally cannot ignore.
translation: 「人人都对魔法感兴趣，」她说，「但不是每个人都能见到魔法师。你难道对我没兴趣吗？」她问着，手指向内勾动示意你过去—这是个你根本无法忽视的召唤。

original: You're pulled inexorably towards her, until your close enough to smell her sweet breath, and further, until you're nestled between her breasts, your body pressed against hers, her arms slowly finding their way around your waist.
translation: 你被不可抗拒地拉向她，直到你近到能闻到她香甜的气息，再进一步，直到你依偎在她的双乳之间，身体紧贴着她，她的手臂慢慢环绕上你的腰。

original: "I... I know you traveled with my mother," you say, looking up from her breasts to her face, as she peers down at you serenly. She smiles. "Didn't you?"
translation: 「我……我知道你和我母亲一起旅行过，」你说着，从她的双乳间抬起头望向她的脸，她正平静地俯视着你。她微微一笑。「难道不是吗？」

original: She runs her hands up your chest, your neck, and clasps one around the back of your neck, and rests the other behind your head. "I did. And do you think she was so oblivious to my charms?"
translation: 她的双手抚上你的胸膛、你的脖颈，一只手扣住你的后颈，另一只手托住你的后脑。「我确实（旅行过）。你觉得她会对我的魅力视而不见吗？」

original: You swallow. Looking into her eyes... you feel... you can't move, you can barely think. Something is stirring beneath her robes... something... alive...
translation: 你咽了口唾沫。凝视着她的双眼……你感觉……你动弹不得，几乎无法思考。有什么东西正在她的法袍下蠢动……某种……活生生的东西……

original: "Rise and shine, boyo," she says, and you realize that at some point, your eyes closed... and, in fact, you're laying down on a long, cushioned seat in her cottage. You feel your body - nothing appears to be missing - and you look around.
translation: “起床了，小伙子，”她说道，你这才意识到，不知何时你已闭上了双眼……事实上，你正躺在她小屋里的一张铺着软垫的长椅上。你感受了一下自己的身体—好像什么也没缺—然后环顾四周。

original: The room is as it was before, but now that you're further in, you can see some of the strange instruments laying about, as well as a ring of iron, the height and width of a person, mounted on a wall.
translation: 房间还是和之前一样，不过现在你身处更靠里的位置，能看到周围散落着一些奇怪的器具，墙上还挂着一个铁环，那铁环刚好能装下一个人大小。

original: You also find the source of the voice, and it's her, the witch, Meri, standing in front of one of the orbs, her eyes closed, her lips curled into a smile.
translation: 你也找到了那道声音的来源—是她，那个女巫，梅丽，正站在一颗水晶球前，闭着双眼，嘴角挂着一丝笑意。

original: She's surrounded by a kind of other-worldly light, her robe lifting in some impossible updraft, but then, it subsides, and the high ringing you had heard dissipates.
translation: 她被某种不属于这个世界的光芒笼罩着，法袍在某种不可能存在的上升气流中飘起，但随后，光芒消散了，你之前听到的那种高亢的嗡鸣声也平静了下来。

original: "You've been asleep a while. I took the liberty of probing how awakened you are. The answer is... not very, but enough. The only question that remains is... what will you give me in exchange?"
translation: “你已经睡了一阵子了。我擅自探测了一下你的觉醒程度。答案嘛……并不高，但足够了。剩下的唯一问题是……你愿意用什么来交换？”

original: You sigh. "I don't have money."
translation: 你叹了口气。“我没钱。”

original: She rolls her eyes. "I don't want money. You have what I want," she says, sitting down next to you, and running her hand up your thigh.
translation: 她翻了个白眼。「我不要钱。你有我想要的东西，」她说着，坐到你旁边，手顺着你的大腿往上滑。

original: You swallow. She nearly gets to your erection, to the point of caressing it through your pants, and stops.
translation: 你咽了口唾沫。她几乎要碰到你的勃起处，隔着裤子抚摸着它，然后停住了。

original: "But... this first spell will be for free. A spell that lets you channel magic into pure flame. Just the kind of thing a mercenary like yourself can use," she says, running her finger down your chest.
translation: 「不过……这第一个咒语是免费的。一个让你把魔力引导成纯净烈焰的咒语。正好适合你这样的佣兵使用，」她说着，手指顺着你的胸膛往下滑。

original: "I'm not a mercenary," you say, and she puckers her lips.
translation: 「我不是佣兵，」你说，她噘起了嘴。

original: "Bounty hunter, then? You're so cute, you know?" she says, leaning forward and almost kissing you, on the lips. You feel... something flow into your body, down your throat, and into your chest - a warmth that spreads, and then a heat that sears.
translation: 「那赏金猎人？你真可爱，你知道吗？」她说着，身体前倾，几乎要吻上你的嘴唇。你感觉到……有什么东西流进你的身体，顺着喉咙往下，进入胸膛—一股暖意扩散开来，然后是一阵灼烧的热度。

original: You pull away, but your throat is too burned to speak or even make a sound, and steam rises from your mouth. You tighten your fists in pain, and feel the fire spread even there, to the tips of your fingers - until it becomes unbearable, and your fists are flung open, your fingers spread, and flames begin to whip around, as if held in the palms of your hands.
translation: 你向后躲开，但喉咙被烧得太厉害，说不出话甚至发不出声音，蒸汽从你嘴里升起。你痛苦地攥紧拳头，感到火焰甚至蔓延到那里，蔓延到指尖—直到无法忍受，你的拳头猛地张开，手指伸展，火焰开始盘旋，仿佛就握在你的掌心中。

original: Panicked, you stumble backwards, thrashing around in the vain hope of extinguishing the flames on your hands - when your eyes catch Meri's gaze. You freeze in place, although you can feel the fire rising in your throat, and you hear a simple command from Meri, though her lips never move.
translation: 惊慌中，你踉跄后退，胡乱摆动，徒劳地希望扑灭手上的火焰—这时你的目光对上了梅里的凝视。你僵在原地，尽管能感到火焰在喉咙里升腾，而且你听到了梅里一个简单的命令，虽然她的嘴唇从未动过。

original: "Swallow it."
translation: 「吞下去。」

original: You close your fists. You swallow as hard as you can, and the fire gets deeper, deeper inside... until it's just a heat again, sitting in your belly.
translation: 你握紧双拳。你用力咽下去，火焰变得更深入、更深地进入体内……直到它又只是一股热意，停留在你的腹中。

original: In the distance, you hear Meri clapping.
translation: 远处，你听到梅里在鼓掌。

original: "Congratulations!" she says, smiling fakely. "Now, you're a true spell-caster. Don't spend it all at once," she says, and you feel ready to pass out. You start to stumble towards the door.
translation: 「恭喜啦！」她说，假惺惺地笑着。「现在，你是个真正的施法者了。别一次全用光哦，」她说，你感觉自己快要昏过去。你开始踉踉跄跄地走向门口。

original: Compared to the class change, this was... substantially less pleasant. You reach the door, and Meri doesn't even try to stop you.
translation: 比起职业转换，这次……实在没那么愉快。你到了门口，梅里甚至没试图拦你。

original: "That won't be all the magic you need, you know. But... it will get you started. I'm sure you'll find your way back to me eventually," she says... and before you know it, you're standing outside of Meri's house, even though you don't recall opening the door. Even stranger... there's fresh snow on the ground.
translation: 「那可不是你需要的全部魔法，你知道的。不过……它能让你起步。我相信你最后总会回到我身边的，」她说……而等你回过神来，你已经站在梅里的房子外面，尽管你不记得自己开了门。更奇怪的是……地上有新鲜的积雪。

original: You learned Combat Fire and True Fire!
translation: 你学会了战斗火焰和真焰！

original: You can now use Incantation to enter a Casting stance!
translation: 你现在可以使用咏唱进入施法姿态！

original: You return, hoping to perhaps learn new magicks... but the mysterious witch is nowhere to be found.
translation: 你返回，希望能学到新的魔法……但那位神秘的魔女已经不见踪影。

original: The forest is quiet, but somehow unfamiliar. The world outside of the small town where you've lived your entire life feels unfamiliar, in fact. The trees are the same here, the ground, the air... but you can't shake the feeling that nothing is the same at all.
translation: 森林很安静，但不知为何透着一股陌生感。事实上，这座小镇以外的整个世界都让你感到陌生。这里的树木、地面、空气都和以前一样……但你无法摆脱那种一切都不再相同的感受。

original: You're finally leaving town... with a weapon in hand, and a class to your name. You feel... unstoppable. You'll gladly face death if it means being able to fulfill your destiny, the one that you've been ignoring for so long.
translation: 你终于要离开小镇了……手里握着武器，头顶着职业头衔。你感觉自己……势不可挡。如果这意味着能实现你长久以来一直忽视的命运，你会欣然面对死亡。

original: You come to a small clearing in the forest... and you hear a cackling. You reach for your scabbard. The voice sounded... almost human. But not quite... and the malice was unmistakable.
translation: 你来到森林中的一小片空地……听到了一阵尖笑声。你伸手握住剑鞘。那声音听起来……几乎像人，但又不完全是……而且那股恶意毫不掩饰。

original: You look around, trying to track the source - your entire body tenses, the pounding of your own pulse drumming your ears, the crunch of the soil underfoot such a burdensome distraction that you keep perfectly still. This is it. Your first battle.
translation: 你环顾四周，试图追踪声音的来源—你的全身都绷紧了，自己的心跳声在耳中砰砰作响，脚下的土壤嘎吱作响，这沉重的干扰让你完全僵住了。就是现在。你的第一场战斗。

original: The scabbard shakes as you grip it, as if you were shaking, but that can't be. Your mind is focused, your spirit is willing, and as soon as you see the fangs and claws, you'll know what to do - or so you think, when suddenly, a figure emerges from behind a fallen log... and you immediately recognize how it had been hiding behind it.
translation: 你紧握着剑鞘，它随之颤抖，就像你在颤抖一样—但那不可能。你的头脑专注，你的精神愿意，等一看到那些尖牙和利爪，你就会知道该怎么做—至少你是这么想着的，突然，一个身影从一根倒下的圆木后面浮现……你立刻明白了它之前是怎么躲在那后面的。

original: It's... a little green goblin. From the proportions, it looks like a female. She's stumbling around, laughing... there's no doubt about it - she's drunk.
translation: 这是……一只小小的绿色哥布林。从体型来看，是雌性。她正踉踉跄跄地走着，笑着……毫无疑问—她喝醉了。

original: "Heya! Hey! You! The boy with the fat butt!" she says, before immediately laughing herself silly, apparently finding the reddening of your ears hilarious. "Why don't... you don't... why don't..." she slurs, and then falls over, her loincloth falling out of place to give you an eyeful. You're apparently not the only one with some junk in the trunk.
translation: 「嘿呀！嘿！你！那个大屁股的小男孩！」她说着，然后立刻把自己笑得前仰后合，显然你发红的耳朵让她觉得好笑极了。「你为啥不……你不……你为啥不……」她含糊不清地说着，然后摔倒在地，兜裆布滑落下来，让你看了个正着。看来你不是唯一一个屁股上有料的人。

original: You start to relax your grip on the hilt of your sword when, in a startling moment of sobriety, she kips up to her feet. Moreover, she pulls out a small dagger and licks the blade.
translation: 你开始放松握剑的手，结果她在一瞬间突然清醒过来，一个鲤鱼打挺站了起来。不仅如此，她还掏出一把小匕首，舔了舔刀刃。

original: "Ya didn't see that!" she says, cutting the air clumsily.
translation: 「你没看到！」她笨拙地挥了挥刀。

original: She's... pathetic. A half pint without even a proper weapon, taking on someone twice her size... while drunk. This couldn't be any easier.
translation: 她真是……可悲。一个连像样武器都没有的半吊子，居然要挑战两倍于她体型的人……而且还在醉酒状态下。这简直易如反掌。

original: "Wanna play with me? Nyaha!"
translation: 「想跟我玩玩吗？喵哈！」

original: Her skin's green, so it's hard to tell, but... it seems like she's blushing. How she wasn't already blushing while drunk like juice or when she flashed her ass at you, you don't know.
translation: 她的皮肤是绿色的，所以很难分辨……但似乎她脸红了。你不知道为什么她喝得像果汁一样醉、还把屁股露给你看时都没脸红，现在却脸红了。

original: You ready your weapon. There's no point in trying to argue with a bratty, maniacal half-pint. If you can subdue her, maybe you can talk some sense into her.
translation: 你准备好武器。跟一个任性的、发狂的小个子争论毫无意义。如果你能制服她，或许还能跟她讲讲道理。

original: She's still, unmoving... until suddenly, she explodes into frenetic action. "Hey, hey! I think I sobered up! What's for dinner? Nyaha!"
translation: 她一动不动……直到突然爆发出一阵狂乱的动作。「嘿，嘿！我觉得我酒醒了！晚餐吃什么？尼亚哈！」

original: You watch her warily as she picks up her things, barely seeming to register that she just lost a vicious battle.
translation: 你警惕地看着她捡起自己的东西，她似乎完全没有意识到自己刚刚输掉了一场恶战。

original: "I thought you were dead!" you cry, and she stops, and cocks her head to look at you, like you're the crazy one.
translation: 「我以为你死了！」你喊道，她停下来，歪着头看着你，好像你才是那个疯了的人。

original: "0 HP doesn't mean 'dead', doofus, or you'd be stepping over adventurer corpses on your way to the adventurer corpse burrito stand next to the adventurer corpse disposal facility," she says, painting a mental image that you neither like nor appreciate.
translation: 「0点HP不代表‘死了’，傻瓜，不然你在前往冒险者尸体处置设施旁边的冒险者尸体卷饼摊的路上，脚下就会踩着冒险者的尸体了。」她描绘出一幅你既不喜欢也不欣赏的画面。

original: "Then... what?" you say, almost speechless.
translation: 「那……是什么？」你几乎说不出话来。

original: "It means you can't fight anymore! There's a pretty big gap between 'out of the fight' and 'dead'! We don't use numbers for that.",
translation: 「意思是你不能再战斗了！‘失去战斗能力’和‘死了’之间差别可大了！我们不会用数字来表示那个。」

original: "But... you're bleeding..."
translation: 「可……你还在流血啊……」

original: "Oh, don't worry about that. You'll trip over as many rocks as stanching agents. And I'm a gobbo, so I've got super healing!" she says, demonstrating by wiping her bloody arm... only to reveal that underneath the blood, her wounds are already bloodless.
translation: 「哦，别担心那个。你绊倒的石头和止血剂一样多。我可是哥布林，有超级愈合能力！」她说着，擦了擦血淋淋的手臂……结果血迹下面，伤口已经不再流血了。

original: You close your eyes. It all makes sense now. Adventurers aren't all stalwart, fearless heroes... they just don't suffer from permadeath. You nod to yourself, silently. You feel like you've already learned so much.
translation: 你闭上眼睛。现在一切都说得通了。冒险者们并非全都坚毅无畏……他们只是不会永久死亡罢了。你默默地点了点头。感觉自己已经学到了不少。

original: "Anyway, ya beat me! How's it gonna be then?"
translation: 「总之，你打败我了！那接下来怎么办？」

original: You blink twice. "How's... what gonna be?"
translation: 你眨了两次眼。「什么……怎么办？」

original: "Nyaha! Don't be coy! How do you like your gobbo - stuffed or filled with cream? Or are you one of those fey boys that wants a buttered up gobbo cock right up the ass?"
translation: 「喵哈！别装糊涂！你喜欢哪种口味的哥布林—是灌满馅儿的还是填满奶油的？还是说你是那种想要涂了黄油的哥布林肉棒直插后穴的妖精灵少年？」

original: "Huh?!" you exclaim, flabbergasted. You have no idea what she's talking about - is she... is she coming on to you?
translation: 「哈？！」你惊得目瞪口呆。完全搞不懂她在说什么—她这是……这是在勾引你吗？

original: "Come on, pinkskin! Whip it out!" she says, slapping her palm against the bulge in her loincloth, apparently indicating what, exactly, you should whip out. "Or I'll whip mine out and give ya a big, fat GAME OVER where it counts!"
translation: "来吧，粉皮！掏出来！"她说着，拍了拍自己兜裆布下鼓起的部位，显然是在示意你到底该掏出什么。"不然我就掏出来，给你来个又大又肥的GAME OVER，让你知道厉害！"

original: You... run. You turn heel and make a break for it. What is she talking about? Why does she just assume...
translation: 你……逃跑了。你转身拔腿就跑。她到底在说什么？她凭什么就这么认为……

original: Your thoughts can barely catch up to you, the stinging of the cuts on your face from the battle and the branches are so much more immediate than the confusion that trails you like the mist off someone emerging from a hot bath into the cool air.
translation: 你的思绪几乎跟不上自己，战斗中脸上被划伤的火辣辣刺痛和树枝的抽打比背后那团困惑更直观—那困惑就像某人从热水澡中出来，踏入冷空气时带出的雾气，紧随身后。
